bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility and shows no concession pattern yet, so I will counter with my strongest values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid while making only a tiny concession to avoid simply repeating my previous offer. The opponent’s two offers are both very low utility for me and their second offer is not an improvement, so there is no evidence yet that they are conceding toward my priorities.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation at t=0.04, but I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply standing still. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE, but their latest offer conceded issueC to my top value, so I keep issueC=valueB while conceding issueD where my weight is relatively low.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early-stage range, since time is still only 0.06 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has mostly pushed valueE, but they have conceded issueC to my top valueB; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while making/maintaining concessions on lower-weight issues.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueC to valueB and issueD/issueE toward valueE, so I keep my high-value positions on issueA and issueB while conceding issueD and issueE to values they have repeatedly signaled.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.76 while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my last bid. The opponent’s offers have improved for me overall, especially by moving issueC to valueB and issueA up to valueB, but they are still holding issueB and issueE at low-utility values for me, so at t=0.10 I should hold firm on my most important issues.

I targeted a utility around 0.70, conceding somewhat from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the most important issueA and issueC values. The opponent’s offers show real concession on issueA and issueC but they remain firm on issueB/issueD/issueE, so I concede lower-weight issueD and issueE first and make a moderate concession on issueB given early time pressure is still low.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.69, conceding only on lower-weight issues while holding my strongest positions on issueA and issueC. The opponent’s offers have clearly conceded on issueA and issueC but remain firm on issueB, issueD, and issueE, so I will move on low-importance issueD/issueE rather than give up too much on issueB this early at t=0.14.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high-utility bid while acknowledging the opponent has been conceding on my high-weight issues, especially issueA and issueC. Their latest offer is already much better for me than their opening bid, but issueB remains important to me, so I will hold issueB at valueB while conceding low-weight issueD and issueE to valueE.

The opponent has conceded substantially on my highest-weight issues (issueA to valueA and issueC to valueB), while still holding issueE at valueE and only partially improving issueB. Since it is still early (t=0.18), I am targeting a utility around 0.69–0.70 and conceding mainly on lower-weight issues rather than giving up issueA or issueC.
